title: Technology Resources: Cybersecurity
description: Conroe ISD's cybersecurity policy outlines how the district protects its technology systems, networks, and the sensitive data stored within them — including student and staff information. It establishes the responsibilities of district staff in preventing, detecting, and responding to cyber threats and security incidents. Keeping these systems secure helps ensure that learning is not disrupted and that personal information remains protected.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's CQB (Local) Cybersecurity policy adds language clarifying the cybersecurity coordinator's role, specifying that the coordinator will oversee implementation of the district's cybersecurity plan and required staff training.
changes_added: who will oversee implementation of the district's cybersecurity plan and required staff training
timeline_local.notes: The only visible redline change is red underlined text in the Coordinator section on page 2. No red strikethrough (removed) text was identified. The cover page title reads 'Discuss and Possibly Adopt Revisions to Board Policy CQB (Local)', which maps to action = 'adopted'.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's CQB (Local) Cybersecurity policy adds language clarifying the role of the cybersecurity coordinator, specifying that they will oversee implementation of the district's cybersecurity plan and required staff training.
changes_added: who will oversee implementation of the district's cybersecurity plan and required staff training
timeline_local.notes: The cover page title reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy CQB (Local)" indicating a first reading. The only redline change visible is red underlined text in the Coordinator section on page 2. No red strikethrough (removed) text was identified. All other policy text appears in black (unchanged).

description_local: This policy establishes the rules and requirements for protecting the school district's technology systems and digital information from cyber threats. It applies to the district as a whole, requiring the designation of a cybersecurity coordinator and the development of a formal cybersecurity plan. This matters to parents and community members because it helps ensure that sensitive student and family data stored by the district is safeguarded against breaches or cyberattacks.
